metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: [Healthcare](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/healthcare/index.html)

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: [David Bolscher](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/david-bolscher/index.html)

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: [Henk Hofstede](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/henk-hofstede/index.html)

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: More and more Dutch people are struggling with a chronic illness. They need care and work less. Companies have a major influence on people's health through their personnel policy, their activities in the living environment and the products they sell to consumers. The tight labor market forces companies to make choices that contribute to the health of society.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: A healthy society is important for well-being and prosperity. Healthy people feel good and usually work more and longer. A structurally tight labor market puts people's health high on the agenda of government and companies. That benefits the economy. For example, the economy will grow by up to [4 percent](https://www.nber.org/papers/w8587) if the population lives one year longer. Healthy years of life contribute to business productivity.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: The importance of health is evident from the significant government expenditure of more than 100 billion euros on healthcare. More than a quarter of the total budget is allocated to healthcare. Double the amount that goes to education. Investing in care is not enough because health is also explained by other factors. For example, the National Institute for Public Health and the Environment (RIVM) estimates that more than 8 percent of the disease burden in the Netherlands can be attributed to unhealthy food, 5 percent to poor working conditions and approximately 3.5 percent to polluted air. Socio-economic and genetic factors are also decisive. Factors over which people themselves have little influence.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Companies influence the health of society through various channels. As employers, companies influence the health of their staff. As producers, companies influence the living environment. And as sellers of products, companies influence the health of consumers. For example, livestock farmers can commit themselves to cleaner air and better nutrition, manufacturers can capture hazardous substances or leave populated areas, retailers can offer healthier products and gyms get people moving. In addition, almost all companies encourage employment and social encounters.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: In the tight labor market, companies need everyone. This forces entrepreneurs to make healthy choices. Entrepreneurs contribute by making the health of their staff, the living environment and consumer products part of their strategy.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: ## More than half of Dutch people are chronically ill

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Despite annual government expenditure of more than 100 billion euros on the healthcare sector, the number of long-term ill people is increasing. Currently, more than 10 million Dutch people are chronically ill, compared to 7.5 million people in 2011, according to [Nivel Zorgregistries](https://www.vzinfo.nl/chronische-opathies-en-multimorbiditeit/age-en-gender). More and more people are suffering from chronic diseases such as cancer, diabetes and cardiovascular disease. Moreover, more than half of chronically ill people suffer from two or more conditions. That makes society less productive. For example, [research](https://www.partnerschapoverbeweging.nl/burden-of-disease-study-of-overweight-and-obesity-nl/) shows that obesity costs the Netherlands 79 billion euros annually, more than half of which is reflected in loss of productivity due to absenteeism and unemployment.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Part of the increase in chronically ill people can be explained by the fact that people are getting older. Older people are more likely to be chronically ill; for example, almost all people over 75 have a chronic condition. Yet the number of chronically ill people is increasing faster than the number of elderly people. The increase in the number of chronically ill people is also because people live unhealthy lives. Economic and social factors such as income and living environment have more influence on people's health than their genes or the quality of care. Economic and social factors determine, among other things, the quality of food that people consume, whether they live in an environment with poor or good air quality and how much they exercise.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Chronic diseases are caused by a combination of factors such as nutrition, exercise, living and working conditions, air pollution, financial concerns and social contacts. While fewer people smoke and drink in the last ten years, mental problems and obesity are increasing sharply. For example, almost 44 percent of Dutch people had feelings of anxiety or depression last year, compared to 36 percent in 2014. In the same period, the number of seriously overweight adults increased from 13.3 to 15.7 percent.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Fortunately, more and more people consider their health important. This is evident, for example, from the sharp decline in the number of smokers and excessive drinkers over the past ten years. The Dutch also indicate en masse that they consider a healthy lifestyle important. More than a third of the population would like to eat healthier and almost all Dutch people consider sport and exercise important, according to a [survey by the Central Bureau of Statistics](https://www.cbs.nl/nl-nl/longread/verslags/2023/on--gezonde-leefstijl-2022-opzichten-motives-en-voeringen?onepage=true#c-6--Overheidsbedrijven--gezondere-voedingskeuzes) (CBS).

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Good intentions do not directly lead to a healthier society. Lifestyle and behavior are difficult to change. In addition, people cannot influence everything. This way they cannot change the air quality. The RIVM and the WHO therefore advise that not only the government, but also companies should make efforts for a healthier society.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Companies influence people's health through their personnel policy, the way they interact with their immediate environment and the quality of the products they sell to consumers. They can invest in better working conditions by, for example, encouraging their staff to have better nutrition, more exercise and more social contacts; in cleaner production that, for example, improves air quality; and in higher-quality products where foods in particular influence health.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: These factors reinforce each other and therefore companies must take all factors into account in their choices and policies. For example, due to high work pressure, an employee may eat unhealthy food and exercise little. Conversely, someone in the supermarket can be tempted to eat unhealthy food, causing someone to sleep worse and not have enough energy to do work.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Government and municipalities also aim for a healthy society. For example, the government can intervene with taxes or subsidies, such as increasing taxes on sugary drinks. Municipalities create a healthy environment with subsidies, tenders and an environmental plan. With policy they reduce the number of suppliers of unhealthy food and air pollution from transport and factories.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Companies that take into account the effects on the health of employees, the environment and consumers in their choices are rewarded. In this way, healthy staff directly contributes to higher productivity and lower absenteeism costs. In addition, companies can retain customers and consumers who consider health important.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: More and more companies are focusing on the health of their staff. In a tight labor market, this is crucial for business growth, as is also evident from our previous report on staff well-being. Well-being is important for attracting and retaining staff and contributes to productivity.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: [Read also: Staff welfare crucial for business growth](/nl/business/insights/sectors-and-trends/sectorprognoses/alle-sectoren/welzijn-staff-cruciaal-voor-bedrijfsgroei.html)

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Yet staff drop out more often. Absenteeism due to illness in the Netherlands fluctuates around 5 percent, while it was around 4 percent ten years ago. This is mainly due to the mental burden. Absenteeism due to illness is the highest in the healthcare sector. Staff succumb to the high workload, incidents and physical strain.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Healthcare providers can protect their staff with workable schedules, innovative tools and psychological support. More leave hours give staff room for relaxation and can increase mental resilience. This investment pays for itself in less absenteeism and higher productivity.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: In other sectors such as industry, transport and construction, a lot of attention is paid to safety in the workplace. Incidents are prevented with clear regulations and training of staff. This not only prevents staff from being absent for long periods of time, it can also save the employer millions of euros in damage claims.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Mental health of staff is also important. Last year, one in five employees suffered from burnout complaints, according to TNO's latest National Working Conditions Survey (NEA). Employees become overstressed due to high work pressure, unsafe working atmosphere or private circumstances. Employers can prevent this by regularly discussing how someone is feeling and relieving staff where necessary. Encouraging movement among staff is important to limit health damage from sitting or standing in a difficult working position for a long time.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Even if the cause lies more privately, an employer can help. In this way, companies can reduce the financial worries of employees, as cleaning company Ruitenheer does by paying off employee debts. Some employers also offer employees a gym membership or access to a psychologist to boost mental health. In addition, more and more workers also provide informal care due to the aging population. Open communication and a flexible attitude of employers help in combining work with care tasks, according to our previous analysis.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text:
- [Employee with financial worries, but employer knows nothing](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/alle-sectoren/werknemer-in-geldzorgen-maar-werkgever-weet-van-niets.html)
- [Avoiding debt among employees as a core value](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/ondernemershalen/zaelijke-diensten/schulden-bij-werknemers-voorkomen-als-kernwaarde.html)
- [More pressure on informal care is also a concern for employers](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/alle-sectoren/meer-druk-op-mantelzorg-ook-een-zorg-voor-werkgever.html)

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Sometimes staff health comes at the expense of price competition. Employees at home or abroad are then paid too little or have to work long hours. They eventually become ill, and in the case of migrant workers, sometimes without a safety net, which means they end up on the street. It can be difficult to offer employees sufficient wages and good working conditions, especially in sectors with high competition and price pressure. Government intervention is then necessary to prevent abuses.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: A healthy environment is important to prevent people from getting sick. A major pathogen is polluted air, partly caused by production and transport movements of companies. An environment can also promote health in other ways. A green environment stimulates exercise and mental well-being. Shopping centers and entertainment areas contribute to social contacts that are also important for health. Companies can therefore create a healthy environment in various ways. The environment in the Netherlands is not healthy in many places. According to the RIVM, 3.5 percent of the disease burden in the Netherlands can be attributed to air pollution. The air is especially polluted near roads, industry, construction sites and livestock farms. Companies can combat a large part of air pollution through fewer and cleaner transport movements of goods and people and with production techniques that reduce emissions. In addition to air pollution, biodiversity loss can harm health through food and water. How companies can limit damage to biodiversity can be read in our previous report.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: [Read also: Billions in damage to biodiversity necessitates radical steps](/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/alle-sectoren/billions in damage-to-biodiversity-necessitates-radical-steps.html)

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Food and water are becoming increasingly contaminated. Chemical substances end up in the soil and water due to waste and pesticides, among other things. People ingest these PFAS through drinking water and food. The amount of PFAS in food in particular can be harmful to health. For example, the RIVM recently warned that people should no longer eat eggs from their hobby chickens. Work is currently underway on a European ban on the use of PFAS agents.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Entrepreneurs in polluting sectors already have to comply with a number of guidelines and regulations. Yet in recent years, the health damage that companies cause to the environment has often been in the news. For example, the RIVM reports on the harmful substances present in the IJmond and the institute links this to a shorter life expectancy of residents in Wijk aan Zee. The risk of asthma in children is also greater there.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Polluting sectors such as construction, industry, energy, food, transport and agriculture must invest in cleaner production. Entrepreneurs are faced with stricter regulations and it is better to anticipate these changes. Service sectors such as healthcare and business services also contribute to a healthy environment with greenery around their buildings and less polluting transport movements.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: The health of consumers is largely determined by providers and producers of food. According to the Nutrition Center, people can become chronically ill from excessive consumption of processed products, sugars and saturated fats. Someone can also become ill due to a lack of healthy nutrients such as fiber, vitamins and minerals.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Retailers, food manufacturers and catering establishments have a direct influence on the health of consumers through the food they make and sell. Some sectors also have an indirect effect, for example the leisure sector that gets people moving and in contact with each other. Or the real estate sector where maintaining and insulating homes contributes to the health of tenants.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Certain products are harmful because people tend to use too much of them. Well-known addiction-prone products are alcohol and tobacco. But food manufacturers also combine ingredients that consumers want to continue eating. Brands are experimenting with advertising strategies to sell more products, and programmers are designing algorithms to keep users looking at a screen for as long as possible. Excessive consumption of certain foods can lead to physical complaints and excessive use of certain applications can lead to mental complaints. Making consumers addicted usually causes health damage.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: The majority of the products on offer in supermarkets and catering establishments do not fall within the Wheel of Five nutritional guideline. In addition, unhealthy food in particular can be purchased at a discount. For example, previous [research](https://nos.nl/artikelen/2404320-80-procent-van-supermarktofferen-stimuleert-ongezonde-voeding) by Wageningen University & Research, among others, shows that more than four in five offers do not appear in the Wheel of Five. The Council of Health and Society (RVS) [advises](https://www.raadrvs.nl/advies/gezond-in-de-bonus/documents/publicaties/2024/12/16/gezond-in-de-bonus) the government to reward providers of healthy food compared to providers of unhealthy food.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Suppliers of food and other products can influence consumer choices. They can increase the range of healthy products and offer healthy food cheaper than unhealthy food. They can also entice consumers to make healthy choices with more advertising for healthy food or with inspiration for healthy snacks and healthy recipes. The government can influence choices by lowering the tax rate on healthy food compared to the rate on unhealthy food.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: Financial service providers also have an important role in consumer health. Debts and financial worries lead to mental and, eventually, physical complaints. The financial sector can counter this with information and clear conditions. Sales methods that increase the risk of financial problems, such as post-payment for consumers, should be avoided. Other companies also play a role here by not offering customers this payment service.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: In addition to making adjustments to their own business operations, entrepreneurs can limit health damage by making health part of their purchasing policy. They can impose requirements on how suppliers deal with the health of their staff, the environment and consumers. In addition, entrepreneurs can give priority to suppliers who are committed to a healthy society.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: A healthy society is necessary for prosperity. It is expected that the government will focus more on health. Only broad social change can reverse the increase in disease burden. Companies play a crucial role in promoting the health of their staff, residents in the living environment and consumers.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society

text: In the coming years, the sector will change to provide more care with fewer resources. The demand for care continues to increase due to an aging population, while it is hardly possible to attract more healthcare personnel. Innovation and collaboration make work easier and more fun, ensuring that healthcare provision remains sustainable and of high quality.

metadata.source_title: Companies take the lead for a healthy society
